reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures the informal statement. The parameterization (O, v) with v ≠ (0,0) ranges over all non-degenerate squares: A = O, B = O+v, C = O+v+Rv, D = O+Rv where R is rotation by 90°, so the four sides all have length |v| and consecutive sides are perpendicular. Every square in the plane arises from some such (O, v). The restriction v ≠ (0,0) is the standard reading of "square" (requiring non-degenerate vertices); allowing v = 0 would trivialize the problem. The function type ℝ × ℝ → ℝ matches "real-valued function on the plane", the sum condition is faithfully transcribed, and the conclusion ∀ P, f P = 0 matches "f(P)=0 for all points P". Wrapping the implication in an iff with the solution (to be set True) correctly formalizes the yes/no question "Does it follow that...".
